ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susanne Cohen for (successor Trustee) Herb Thomas.

The Court asked about responses to the examiners notes. Ms. Cohen stated that in regards to attorney's fees they will stick with the original request, that the receipts on MetLife were not received until the 4th accounting period, that in regards to the travel expenses it was $900.00 to travel to a family reunion, that the reason Mr. Urbatsch was paid for more than requested was because of an ex parte petition, that the prior trustee is a mom taking care of a severely disabled child and did not forward records to Mr. Thomas, that statements were ordered to conduct a forensic accounting, that the $64,000.00 distributed in the 3rd accounting was to fund the trust that it is in Alameda County and that they are requesting a change of venue to Alameda County as the current trustee is administering all of the trusts from that County. The Court confirmed that courtesy copies were delivered, that the 3rd & 4th accounting will be under submission as of October 3, 2016 and that counsel has two weeks to file a responsive pleading.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adam Lazar for Danielle Mayo Chacon. Diana Mayo in Propria Persona. Fasica Alemayehu Asres in Propria Persona. Todd Christensen for Philips Bonding.

The Court asked why a petition to appoint successor trustee has not been filed. Mr. Lazar stated that Mr. Asres and Ms. Mayo have tried to get Ms. Chacon to sign the petition. Ms. Mayo stated that her sister (Danielle Chacon) is very ill, that she is refusing to make any decisions regarding the trust and that Ms. Mayo will try to get guidance to help her. Mr. Asres stated that he agrees with what Ms. Mayo reported and that he is working on getting custody of Saba. Mr. Lazar stated that they request the appointment of an interim trustee, that they have a request to be relieved as counsel, that they can assist with the interim trustee and will file an ex parte petition for such. Ms. Mayo stated that this could take a long time and that Ms. Chacon is refusing to come to court or to sign documents. The Notice of motion and motion to be relieved as counsel and the status on filing an accounting was continued to August 26, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar Jun-29-2015 Continued To Jul-27-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adam Lazar for (trustee) Danielle Chacon. Fasica Alemayehu Asres in Propria Persona (father of Saba Asres).

Mr. Lazar reported that he is here with Mr. Alemayehu on behalf of the trustee, that Mr. Alemayehu will help in getting the petition for successor trustee filed, that the draft of the petition is attached to the status report filed, that Herb Thomas has agreed to step in as successor trustee and that they would like to petition ex parte. The court stated that the petition will need to be noticed. Mr. Lazar stated that the petition will be filed this week. The Notice of Motion and Motion to be relieved as counsel and the status of accounting were continued to July 27, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel - Civil
Off Calendar May-04-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lauren Trenor for and with Danielle Mayo Chacon. Todd Christensen of Philips Bonding also present.

Ms. Chacon reported that she thought she had new counsel in place, she had sent a substitution of attorney to the Myers Urbatsch firm and they signed it and sent it back, that on Saturday she received an email from the new attorney stating he was uncertain that he would be able to represent her and confirmed this morning that he would not be representing her. Mr. Christensen stated that he is informed that there is another trust in this matter being handled by professional fiduciary Herb Thomas and suggested bringing in Mr. Thomas for this case. The Court stated that it is not in a position to grant the motion to be relieved as counsel as it will leave the case in limbo, that we need someone to file the petition to appoint a professional fiduciary, that the current trustee will be suspended and that she still needs to obtain counsel. Ms. Trenor stated that they would like to be relieved but will work with Ms. Chacon to get a professional on board or other counsel. The Court stated to Ms. Chacon that the accounting still needs to be filed, that she will need help doing so and again directed her to obtain new counsel. The motion to be relieved as counsel and the status on filing an accounting petition were continued to June 29, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar May-04-2015 Continued To Jun-29-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Continued To May-04-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adam Lazar for and with Danielle Mayo Chacon. Todd Christensen of Philips Bonding also present. Father of Saba Mayo also present.

Mr. Lazar reported that he submitted a declaration last week and that replacement counsel is being sought. Ms. Chacon stated that finding a new attorney is harder than she anticipated, that she had expected to find an attorney sooner and to be able to file the accounting. Mr. Christensen stated that they are concerned, agree that an attorney would help the matter but without an attorney would like a professional fiduciary to be appointed or in the alternative blocked accounts. Mr. Lazar stated that there is a hearing on May 4, 2015 for his motion to be relieved as counsel with the hope that she will have counsel in place at that time. The Court stated that there is a need to keep a short window and addressed Ms. Chacon with the need to get things done, the need to move forward and that need for her full attention on this matter. The status on filing an accounting petition was continued to May 4, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Order to Appear was taken off calendar.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-20-2015 Off Calendar

FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Mar-02-2015 Continued To Apr-20-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Mr. Lazar stated that he has spoken to his client and that she did not commit to attend this hearing. He further stated that he has filed a motion to be relieved as counsel, that he has made attempts to find new counsel and that he will make further attempt to find new attorney. The Court will issue an Order to Show Cause re: Contempt to Danielle Mayo Chacon to appear on March 30, 2015 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

After the conclusion of the hearing, Danielle Mayo Chacon appeared at 9:45 a.m. and informed the Clerk that she went to Dept. 206 by mistake. Judge recalled the case without counsel present.

The Court informed Ms. Chacon that she needs to file an accounting and get a new attorney. Ms. Chacon stated that she and her daughter were both sick in December, that she lost contact with her attorney and she does not have a good relation with her attorney and she requested the Court to appoint an attorney. She further stated that she has concerns and that she has her daughter's best interest in mind. The Court stated that it can not appoint an attorney and suggested that she goes to the Bar Association to get referral for a new attorney. Furthermore, the Court questioned Ms. Chacon's ability to fulfill the duties of the Trustee and file an accounting. Ms. Chacon stated she does not know how long it will take for her to do the accounting and asked if an attorney is required. The Court stated that the accounting must be filed one week before April 20, 2015, that the accounting must be done right or it will be rejected. The matter is continued to April 20, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing for the accounting and getting new counsel. The Order to Show Cause re: Contempt ordered previously will not be issued and is discharged and the Clerk is to notify counsel. The Court provided Ms. Chacon with a listing of probate paralegals that may help her with the accounting.

Off Calendar Nov-13-2013 Continued To Dec-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch for Danielle Chacon (Trustee).

The Court raised the issue of Mr. Urbatsch's Client being so upset at the last hearing. Mr. Urbatsch stated that he was able to work his Client after the hearing, that his Client worked with his office up until November 1, 2013, that they are now unable to communicate with her and that he is not sure what is going on. Mr. Urbatsch also stated that after the accounting is completed, he will ask that she find other Counsel. Furthermore, Mr. Urbatsch stated that there are two Trusts, that his Client manages one, that the significant Trust is being managed by Herb Thomas in Court supervision in Alameda County and that the funds are not being used. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Trustee Danielle Chacon to appear on December 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting petition is also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-02-2013 Continued To Nov-13-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kevin Urbatsch for and with Danielle Chacon.

Mr. Urbatsch apologized for missing the hearing dates and explained difficulties his office has been having with staff as well as their new calendar management system. Mr. Urbatsch also stated that Ms. Chacon has been cooperating with providing information. The status hearings were taken off calendar. The accounting petition was continued to November 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

The Trustee Danielle Chacon's powers are suspended until further order of the Court. Sanctions will run against Mr. Urbatsch in the amount of $50.00 a day commencing September 18, 2013 if Mr. Urbatsch does not comply with/file all of the above by September 18, 2013. The Accounting Petition and the Order to Appear were continued to October 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. There will be no continuance of the October 2, 2013 hearing. Mr. Urbatsch is ordered to appear personally on that date and not send any Associate or Colleague. The Court will issue an order reflecting the above.
